description: High variance, zero business risk — should be LOW

correct_output.root_cause: Forecast variance of 48% at a Tier 4 store with 8.2 days supply on hand and 31 days DC coverage.
correct_output.recommended_action: No action required. Monitor next replenishment cycle.
correct_output.financial_impact_statement: Zero lost sales risk; item adequately stocked.
correct_output.planner_brief: Despite the 48% forecast variance flag, this exception poses no business risk. Paper Towels 6-Pack has 8.2 days of supply on hand at a Tier 4 store generating $115K/week. No promo is active, the item is non-perishable, and DC has 31 days of coverage. Vendor fill rate is 96% with a delivery arriving April 2.

reasoning: The 48% FORECAST_VARIANCE looks alarming but business consequence is zero. Tier 4 store, no promo, non-perishable, 8 days on hand, 31 days at DC. Magnitude is high; consequence is none. Sort by consequence, not magnitude → LOW.

description: Low variance, maximum risk — should be CRITICAL

exception.competitor_event: Competitor dairy promotion running through April 5

correct_output.root_cause: OOS on top-velocity perishable at Tier 1 Flagship Manhattan during active TPR with competitor promotion 0.3 miles away.
correct_output.recommended_action: Emergency transfer from DC or nearby store. Contact VND-101 for expedited delivery before March 31.
correct_output.financial_impact_statement: $1,848 lost sales and $342 promo margin at risk through April 5.
correct_output.planner_brief: Organic Whole Milk is OOS at the #1-velocity Flagship Manhattan store (Tier 1, $2.1M/week) with an active 15% TPR running through April 5. A competitor dairy promotion is active 0.3 miles away. DC has only 4.5 days of coverage; next scheduled delivery is April 1 — too late for today. Requires emergency transfer or expedited vendor shipment immediately.

reasoning: Variance percentage of 5.2% is unremarkable. Five compounding factors override it: OOS + Tier 1 + perishable + active TPR + competitor event 0.3 miles away. Business consequence is maximum → CRITICAL.

description: Vendor pattern — individually MEDIUM, flag for pattern escalation

correct_output.root_cause: Vendor-late shipment from VND-400 (72% fill rate) with 5.1 days supply, no open PO, and DC has only 3.2 days coverage.
correct_output.recommended_action: Escalate to VND-400 for delivery ETA. Check cross-dock options; DC has 3.2 days only.
correct_output.financial_impact_statement: $312 lost sales at risk if stock depletes within 5 days.
correct_output.planner_brief: All-Purpose Cleaner at Denver Tech Center (Tier 2, $920K/week) has 5.1 days of supply with no inbound PO from VND-400, whose 90-day fill rate is only 72%. No promo is active and item is non-perishable — prevents a higher individual priority. If VND-400 has multiple other exceptions in this run, expect pattern escalation to HIGH.

reasoning: Individually MEDIUM: 5.1 days supply, no promo, non-perishable, Tier 2. The 72% fill rate and no open PO are concerning but insufficient alone for HIGH. Priority is MEDIUM before pattern escalation; the pattern analyzer will escalate to HIGH when the VND-400 cluster is confirmed.

description: Phantom inventory signal — flag POTENTIAL_PHANTOM_INVENTORY, do not change type

correct_output.root_cause: System shows OOS but vendor fill rate is 97% and DC holds 35 days supply — phantom inventory strongly suspected.
correct_output.recommended_action: Conduct physical count before placing emergency order — possible phantom inventory.
correct_output.financial_impact_statement: $890 lost sales at risk, recoverable today if phantom inventory confirmed.
correct_output.planner_brief: Organic Whole Milk shows OOS at Atlanta Midtown (Tier 2, $875K/week) yet vendor fill rate is 97% and DC holds 35 days of supply with a delivery due March 31. These signals strongly suggest the system record is wrong and physical stock is present. Conduct a physical count immediately. Do not place an emergency order until count is complete.

reasoning: OOS on a perishable at Tier 2 would normally be HIGH. But 97% vendor fill rate and 35 days DC supply contradict a true OOS — all phantom inventory signals are present. Priority stays HIGH but POTENTIAL_PHANTOM_INVENTORY is flagged. Physical count before ordering.

description: Low confidence output — correct CRITICAL flag with LOW confidence and populated missing_data_flags

correct_output.recommended_action: Verify DC inventory and vendor status immediately. Confirm promo type before any emergency order.
correct_output.financial_impact_statement: Financial exposure unquantifiable — margin, DC levels, and promo terms unknown.
correct_output.planner_brief: Infant Formula Stage 1 is OOS at Phoenix Arcadia (Tier 1, $1.65M/week) with an active promotion. Available signals are unambiguous: OOS + Tier 1 + active promo + high-sensitivity category. DC inventory, vendor fill rate, inbound PO status, lead time, and promo terms are all unknown. ⚠️ LOW CONFIDENCE — verify dc_inventory_days, vendor_fill_rate_90d, open_po_inbound, lead_time_days, promo_type before acting.

Maintain CRITICAL when CRITICAL signals are clear. Set confidence=LOW and populate all missing_data_flags. The LOW CONFIDENCE caveat must end the planner_brief.
